text: Glycinium Sulfamate–Glycine - An organic ionic molecular cocrystal demonstrating piezoelectric response through engineered non-centrosymmetric crystal structure, combining glycine, sulfamic acid, and glycinium cation in a functional ionic crystal
intermolecular_interactions: Charge-assisted N-H···O hydrogen bonds between glycinium ammonium and sulfamate anion
O-H···O hydrogen bonds from sulfamate hydroxyl group
Hydrogen bonding tapes sustained by sulfamate N-H···O interactions
Ionic interactions between glycinium cations and sulfamate anions
experimental_method: Piezoresponse force microscopy (PFM), Second harmonic generation (SHG), Commercial d33 piezoelectric meter
computational_method: Density functional theory (DFT) using VASP with PBE GGA functional, density functional perturbation theory (DFPT) for piezoelectric tensor
shear_qualitative: Moderate piezoelectric response in transverse direction
longitudinal_qualitative: Low to moderate longitudinal piezoelectric response
longitudinal_unit: pC/N (d33 coefficient for single crystal; polycrystalline ≥0.2 pC/N)
dft_shear_qualitative: DFT-predicted transverse constant maximum d12 = -3.7 pC/N
property_ref_title: A Piezoelectric Ionic Cocrystal of Glycine and Sulfamic Acid

additional_properties.cocrystal_composition: 2:1 glycine to sulfamic acid ionic cocrystal
additional_properties.protonation_state: One glycine molecule protonated by sulfamic acid to form glycinium cation

shg_measurements.comparison: Higher SHG response than KDP, lower than urea (1.04 pm/V)

supramolecular_architecture.cation_motif: Three-component charge-assisted hydrogen-bonded motif involving ammonium group of adjacent glycinium cations
supramolecular_architecture.anion_motif: Hydrogen-bonded tapes sustained by N-H···O bonds in sulfamate anions
supramolecular_architecture.crystal_packing: Non-centrosymmetric due to observed motifs precluding center of inversion

comparison_with_parent_compounds.significance: Cocrystallization alone induces non-centrosymmetry without requiring non-centrosymmetric parent compounds

polycrystalline_film_characterization.self_assembly: Forms dense films on copper substrates
polycrystalline_film_characterization.crystal_array: Criss-crossing arrays with ~700 nm height variations
polycrystalline_film_characterization.damping_factor: 1 order of magnitude reduction from single crystal response

potential_applications: Autonomous ambient-powered devices in edge computing

distinctive_features.feature_1: First example where centrosymmetric components form non-centrosymmetric cocrystal
distinctive_features.feature_2: Cocrystallization creates new functionality absent in parent compounds
distinctive_features.feature_4: Demonstrates utility of polycrystalline film growth for soft organic crystal characterization

notes: Crystal structure obtained from the Cambridge Structural Database (CSD)
CSD Refcode: IBUHEW, CCDC Deposition Number: 2090689
Uniquely, both pure glycine and pure sulfamic acid crystallize independently in centrosymmetric space groups
Cocrystallization induces non-centrosymmetric Pn space group solely through coformer interactions
Non-centrosymmetric space group Pn (No. 7) provides 10 nonzero piezoelectric strain constants
Crystal structure reveals 2:1 glycine to sulfamic acid stoichiometry with one glycine protonated
Glycinium cations form charge-assisted hydrogen-bonded motifs through ammonium groups
Sulfamate anions form hydrogen-bonded tapes, both precluding center of inversion
Inversion center absence is crucial for understanding cocrystal formation from first principles design
This is a demonstration that cocrystallization of achiral coformers can create non-centrosymmetric structures
Piezoelectric response is novel among amino acid crystals - longitudinal d33 response of ~2 pC/N
Only γ-glycine polymorph previously crystallized in space group permitting nonzero d33 (10 pC/N single crystal)
Polycrystalline film approach overcomes challenges of measuring soft organic crystal electromechanical properties
DFT predictions of piezoelectric tensor guided experimental SHG and PFM measurements
SHG used as suitable noncontact characterization technique for organic crystal coatings
Polycrystalline films demonstrate damped but nonzero macroscopic response (≥0.2 pC/N) - range exploited in quartz, AlN, ZnO technologies
Electronic contribution to d33 value yields 0.57 pm/V - indicates full response primarily from ionic deformation
Hydrogen-bond networks facilitate dipole moment changes during crystal deformation necessary for piezoelectricity
Piezoelectric response generally larger in directions where Young's modulus is small
Control experiments confirm sulfamic acid films alone exhibit zero piezoelectric response (centrosymmetric)
Paper reports both crystal structure determination and complete experimental piezoelectric characterization
Material demonstrates potential for applications in flexible electronics and biocompatible devices
Represents simple, widely applicable means of expanding technology applications of biopiezoelectric materials
